fact: Rescue teams searched for eight days before calling off the search
source: Alive: The Story of the Andes Survivors, Piers Paul Read (1974)

fact: The white fuselage was invisible against the snow, hindering search efforts
source: Alive: The Story of the Andes Survivors, Piers Paul Read (1974)

fact: Stranded at twelve thousand feet (approximately 3,600 meters) in the Andes
source: The crash site was at approximately 11,800 feet (3,570 m). Widely documented; 12,000 feet is an approximate but reasonable figure.

fact: An avalanche hit the fuselage while survivors were sleeping, killing eight people
source: Alive: The Story of the Andes Survivors, Piers Paul Read (1974). The avalanche on October 29, 1972 killed eight survivors.

fact: Two survivors (Nando Parrado and Roberto Canessa) hiked ten days through the mountains to find help
source: Miracle in the Andes, Nando Parrado (2006). The trek lasted approximately 10 days (December 12–22, 1972).

fact: Sixteen survived out of forty-five passengers and crew
source: Widely documented. The aircraft carried 45 people; 16 were ultimately rescued on December 22–23, 1972.
